title: Trirostellum cardiospermum
uses: Unfortunately, I couldn't find any information on Trirostellum cardiospermum. However, I can tell you about a similar plant called Cardiospermum halicacabum, commonly known as balloon vine or heartseed. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Trirostellum cardiospermum is not recognized in the standard botanical literature, but it's possible that it's a misspelling or a lesser-known synonym for Cardiospermum halicacabum. The medicinal uses of Cardiospermum halicacabum include treating inflammation, fever, and other conditions, although the effectiveness and safety of these uses have not been extensively scientifically studied.

Its seeds are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help in treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant is also believed to have antifungal properties, which could aid in the treatment of fungal infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Trirostellum cardiospermum for therapeutic purposes.
